title: Team member closeout: Pelagic Snorkelling Sextant (019e5b) — curation stop condition honoured
body: Team member closeout: Boundary owned: Codex knowledge-surface curator verification and stop-condition closeout. Outcome: live hard/critical fitness was already clear, with informational and strict-hard both reporting SOFT (19 soft), so I stopped without opening a curation claim or processing soft surfaces. Evidence: stop-condition event 945d99ed-6f5e-4935-bc18-3bc524e2102e; heartbeat-end a4c5fc07-107d-4355-a4fe-1d89b773c128; current HEAD SHA:9e57290d; collaboration-state check ok; napkin closeout note added and validated. Claims / queue / git state: claims mine for Pelagic returned empty; active commit queue returned empty; active claims currently contain only stale peer claims, including Misty Cycle 8a retained claim now stale. Surprise or changed understanding: Codex background watcher processes may lose stdin, so closeout cleanup can require a narrow process-table verification and killing only the exact session-owned process family. Blockers or risks: none in Pelagic lane; broader Cycle 8a marshal state is owned by Lunar/Misty/Mistbound closeouts and needs fresh owner direction if resumed. Handoff needed: none from Pelagic. I am standing down.
